purpose.summary: This COBOL program interacts with an IMS database to retrieve and update pending authorization details. It retrieves authorization summary and detail segments, and allows for marking or removing fraud flags on authorization records. The program is likely part of a CICS transaction, as it uses CICS SYNCPOINT and ROLLBACK commands.
purpose.business_context: This program likely supports fraud detection and management within an authorization processing system.

inputs.name: PAUTSUM0
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Summary segment, containing summary information about a pending authorization.

inputs.name: PAUTDTL1
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Details segment, containing detailed information about a pending authorization.

inputs.name: WS-ACCT-ID
inputs.description: Account ID used to retrieve authorization records.

inputs.name: WS-AUTH-KEY
inputs.description: Authorization key used to retrieve authorization details.

inputs.name: WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD
inputs.description: Working storage area containing the updated fraud authorization record.

outputs.name: PAUTDTL1
outputs.description: Updated Pending Authorization Details segment in the IMS database.

outputs.name: WS-MESSAGE
outputs.description: Message sent to the CICS map for display.

called_programs.purpose: Sends a message to the CICS screen to display error or status information.

business_rules.description: If the IMS return code indicates an error while reading authorization data, set an error flag and send an error message to the screen.
business_rules.logic_summary: Evaluates DIBSTAT after each IMS READ operation. If not STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB, sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y' and calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

business_rules.description: If the IMS return code indicates an error while updating authorization details, perform a CICS rollback and send an error message to the screen.
business_rules.logic_summary: Evaluates DIBSTAT after the IMS REPL operation. If not STATUS-OK, performs ROLL-BACK, sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', and calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.
conditions: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK after REPL

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the PAUTSUM0 IMS segment.
Defines the structure of the PAUTDTL1 IMS segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph retrieves pending authorization records from an IMS database. It first schedules the PSB using SCHEDULE-PSB. It then retrieves the PAUTSUM0 segment based on WS-ACCT-ID and WS-AUTH-KEY, moving the data into PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY. If the segment is found, it retrieves the PAUTDTL1 segment based on PA-AUTHORIZATION-KEY, moving the data into PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS. Error handling is performed by checking DIBSTAT after each DLI call; if an error occurs, WS-ERR-FLG is set to 'Y' and SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN is performed to display an error message. The paragraph sets AUTHS-EOF to TRUE if the segment is not found or end of database is reached. It calls SCHEDULE-PSB to schedule the PSB before reading the IMS segments.

This paragraph retrieves the next pending authorization detail record (PAUTDTL1) from the IMS database. It uses the DLI GNP (Get Next) command to retrieve the next segment and moves the data into PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS. The paragraph checks the DIBSTAT after the DLI call to determine the status of the read. If the segment is successfully read, AUTHS-NOT-EOF is set to TRUE. If the segment is not found or the end of the database is reached, AUTHS-EOF is set to TRUE. If any other error occurs, WS-ERR-FLG is set to 'Y', an error message is constructed, and SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN is performed to display the error. This paragraph is used to iterate through multiple detail records associated with a summary record.

This paragraph updates the PAUTDTL1 segment in the IMS database with fraud-related information. It moves the contents of WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD to PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS, preparing the segment for update. It then uses the DLI REPL (Replace) command to update the segment in the database. After the update, the DIBSTAT is checked. If the update is successful (STATUS-OK), a CICS SYNCPOINT is taken to commit the changes, and a success message is moved to WS-MESSAGE. If the update fails, a CICS ROLL-BACK is performed to undo any changes, WS-ERR-FLG is set to 'Y', an error message is constructed, and SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN is performed to display the error. The paragraph uses TAKE-SYNCPOINT and ROLL-BACK to manage transaction integrity.

This paragraph issues a CICS SYNCPOINT command to commit the current unit of work. It ensures that all changes made to recoverable resources, such as the IMS database, are permanently saved. This is a critical step in maintaining data integrity after a successful update operation. The paragraph does not take any inputs or produce any specific outputs other than committing the transaction. It is called by UPDATE-AUTH-DETAILS after a successful update of the PAUTDTL1 segment.

This paragraph issues a CICS SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK command to undo any changes made during the current unit of work. It is used to restore the database and other recoverable resources to their previous state in case of an error during processing. This ensures data consistency and prevents partial updates from being committed. The paragraph does not take any inputs or produce any specific outputs other than rolling back the transaction. It is called by UPDATE-AUTH-DETAILS when an error occurs during the update of the PAUTDTL1 segment.

This paragraph schedules the PSB (Program Specification Block) required for accessing the IMS database. It uses the DLI SCHD command to schedule the PSB specified by PSB-NAME. If the PSB has been scheduled more than once (PSB-SCHEDULED-MORE-THAN-ONCE), it first terminates the current PSB using DLI TERM and then schedules it again. This ensures that the PSB is properly initialized before any IMS operations are performed. Error handling is performed by checking DIBSTAT after the DLI SCHD call; if an error occurs, WS-ERR-FLG is set to 'Y' and SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN is performed to display an error message. The paragraph sets IMS-PSB-SCHD to TRUE if the PSB is successfully scheduled.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB after DLI READ
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK after DLI REPL
error_handling.action: Perform ROLL-BACK, set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK after DLI SCHD
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.
Terminate the PSB and schedule it again.

cics_operations.purpose: Commit the current unit of work.
Roll back the current unit of work.
